Tests the behavior of `Number.prototype.toFixed` when given a non-numeric string argument.

# Purpose
This code is a test assertion that checks the behavior of the `toFixed` method on the `Number` prototype in JavaScript. It verifies that when a non-numeric string argument, `"some string"`, is passed to `toFixed`, the method returns the string `"0"`. This test ensures that the `toFixed` method handles invalid input by defaulting to zero decimal places, which is consistent with the ECMAScript specification. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of the `toFixed` method's behavior.
